markdownRemark.html:
In addition to basic data display, tables also have data filtering requirements. The Table component in Choerodon UI has multiple built-in filter bars, which can be configured easily to achieve table filtering while also allowing customization of query field components.

Attribute with the Same Name - DataSet Props: queryFields

Before introducing the queryFields attribute of Table, let's review the configuration of the Table filter bar:

  1. Instantiate a DataSet and configure query fields

    • The property for configuring query fields in DataSet is queryFields, described in the documentation as: "An array of query field properties, which internally generates a queryDataSet. It has higher priority over the queryDataSet property."
    • The queryFields attribute type in DataSet is the same as the fields attribute, both being FieldProps[]. The fields attribute is used to generate table data columns, while queryFields is used to generate query fields for the table.
  2. Associate the Table component with the DataSet instance
  3. Set the built-in filter bar of the Table component to: filterBar
// DataSet queryFields example
const App = () => {
  const userDs = new DataSet({
    primaryKey: 'userid',
    name: 'user',
    autoQuery: true,
    // Configure Query Fields
    queryFields: [
      { name: 'name', type: 'string', label: '姓名' },
      { name: 'age', type: 'number', label: '年龄' },
      { name: 'code', type: 'object', label: '代码描述', lovCode: 'LOV_CODE' },
    ],
    fields: [
      { name: 'userid', type: 'string', label: '编号', required: true },
      { name: 'name', type: 'string', label: '姓名' },
      { name: 'age', type: 'number', label: '年龄', max: 100, step: 1 },
    ],
    // ...
  });

  return (
    <Table dataSet={userDs} queryBar='filterBar'>
      <Table.Column name='userid' />
      <Table.Column name='name' />
      <Table.Column name='age' />
    </Table>
  );
}

Default Rendering Component Relationship for DataSet Query Fields

The built-in filter bar in Table will automatically match components for rendering based on the type and other attributes of each field in the DataSet's queryFields property. The matching relationships are as follows:

  • lovCode => Lov
  • lookupCode => Select
  • type:number => NumberField
  • type:date => DatePicker
  • type:dateTime => DatePicker[mode=dateTime]
  • type:week => DatePicker[mode=week]
  • default => TextField

Additionally, these components will be passed relevant properties such as record, name, etc.

An example of the above is shown in the figure below:

According to the **queryFields information configured in the DataSet, automatic matching of components can basically satisfy 90% of business scenarios. However, there are some limitations, such as:**

  • The input component is fixed based on its type
  • It is inconvenient to add additional properties

Modifying Default Query Components for Table queryFields

To address the limitations mentioned above, you can customize the query field components by setting the queryFields property of the Table component. When developers want to control the rendering of query fields themselves, they can do so through this property.

The type of the property is: { [key: string]: ReactElement };

Where ReactElement refers to input-type components.

Modifying the Default Rendering of Query Components

For example: changing a number-type field to a currency component

<Table
  dataSet={userDs}
  queryBar='filterBar'
  queryFields={{
    age: <Currency currency="CNY" precision={2} />
  }}
>
  <Table.Column name='userid' />
  <Table.Column name='name' />
  <Table.Column name='age' />
</Table>

Add extra attributes to the query component

For example: you can add a class name to the query component

<Table
  dataSet={userDs}
  queryBar='filterBar'
  queryFields={{
    name: <TextField className='xxx' />,
  }}
>
  <Table.Column name='userid' />
  <Table.Column name='name' />
  <Table.Column name='age' />
</Table>
